trigger: Enterprise account submits a Priority 1 or Priority 2 support ticket, OR a CSM is directly contacted (email, phone, Slack) about a service issue by an account stakeholder.

tasks.name: Receive and triage escalation
tasks.description: CSM receives notification of escalation via Zendesk alert email or direct contact from account stakeholder.
inputs: Direct stakeholder message (email, phone, or Slack)
tasks.automation_notes: Triage can be automated for tickets with clear priority indicators. Escalations that arrive via direct contact or non-standard channels require human judgment to recognize and route.

tasks.name: Pull account profile from CRM
tasks.description: CSM navigates to Salesforce, searches for the account, and manually reviews account health score, contract tier, renewal date, account owner, recent activity history, and any open opportunities.
tasks.automation_notes: Deterministic lookup. No judgment required. Can be triggered automatically when escalation is received.

integration_gap.description: Zendesk does not pull account CRM data when a ticket is created. CSM must manually look up CRM context in a separate tab. Account health score and contract tier are not visible in Zendesk.
integration_gap.downstream_assembly_work: 8 min per escalation × 18 escalations/month = 144 minutes/month per CSM

tasks.name: Retrieve contract terms from DocuSign
tasks.description: CSM navigates to DocuSign, searches for the account's executed Master Service Agreement, identifies the relevant SLA addendum, and manually extracts: response time SLA, uptime guarantee, credit terms, renewal date, and any special provisions.
outputs: Special provisions (e.g., dedicated support, custom SLA windows)
tasks.automation_notes: Contract terms are static, structured, and stored digitally. Can be indexed and retrieved automatically.
tasks.error_notes: CSMs occasionally pull the wrong contract version (e.g., original MSA instead of current amendment). Estimated error rate: ~8% of cases.

integration_gap.description: Contract terms (SLA tier, uptime %, credit provisions) are not surfaced in Salesforce or Zendesk. CSM must manually retrieve from DocuSign each time. There is no automated extraction of contract metadata into the CRM.
integration_gap.downstream_assembly_work: 12 min per escalation × 18 escalations/month = 216 minutes/month per CSM
integration_gap.error_cost: 8% error rate on contract lookup. If wrong SLA tier is used to frame response, CSM may over-promise (credit risk) or under-serve (churn risk). Estimated 1-2 cases/month affected.

tasks.name: Check related open tickets in Zendesk
tasks.description: CSM searches Zendesk for all open and recently closed tickets associated with the account. Reviews ticket titles, statuses, assigned agents, and any engineering escalation flags.
inputs: Account name or Zendesk organization ID
outputs: List of open tickets with status
Pattern of recent issues (e.g., repeated billing errors, recurring API timeout)
Any tickets already assigned to engineering or tier-2 support
tasks.automation_notes: Retrieval is deterministic. Pattern detection (e.g., 'this is the 3rd timeout issue this month') could be flagged automatically.

integration_gap.description: Open tickets are not visible in the CRM account view. Salesforce shows activity logs from CSM interactions but not the underlying Zendesk ticket queue. CSM has to maintain mental model of ticket state across both systems.
integration_gap.downstream_assembly_work: 10 min per escalation × 18 escalations/month = 180 minutes/month per CSM

tasks.name: Identify internal specialist or escalation owner
tasks.description: Based on the issue type (billing, API, data sync, performance, feature), CSM identifies the correct internal team or named specialist and checks their availability in Slack.
tasks.automation_notes: Routing by issue type is rule-based and automatable. However, selection of a specific specialist (vs. the team queue) involves relationship context — the CSM may know that a particular engineer has account history or that a particular tier-2 agent has worked with this client before.
tasks.judgment_notes: Choosing between 'put this in the general queue' vs. 'loop in Marcus specifically because he knows this client' is a relationship judgment, not a rules-based decision.

tasks.name: Draft internal context email to specialist
tasks.description: CSM writes an email or Slack message to the identified specialist summarizing: account context, issue description, contract SLA, account health, history of related issues, and the CSM's assessment of urgency and churn risk.
tasks.automation_notes: Boilerplate context (account health, contract tier, open tickets) can be auto-populated into a template. The CSM's assessment of urgency and churn risk requires judgment and should remain human-authored.
tasks.judgment_notes: CSM's churn risk signal is often the most valuable part of this message — it tells the specialist whether this is a routine technical issue or a relationship-defining moment.

tasks.name: Conduct client call
tasks.description: CSM calls or video conferences with the account stakeholder(s). Listens to the issue, acknowledges the impact, reads between the lines for dissatisfaction signals beyond the stated problem, negotiates interim remediation, and sets clear expectations for resolution.
outputs: Clear understanding of client's emotional state and relationship risk
tasks.automation_notes: This is the irreplaceable work. Empathy, active listening, relationship navigation, reading subtext, negotiating under pressure — none of this is automatable at the level needed to manage enterprise accounts.

tasks.name: Draft response email to client
tasks.description: CSM writes a follow-up email summarizing what was discussed on the call, what has been committed, and next steps. Must match the tone set on the call and not over- or under-commit.
tasks.automation_notes: Structure (recap, commitments, next steps) can be templated. Tone calibration and the specific language used for sensitive accounts requires human judgment.

tasks.name: Log interaction in Salesforce CRM
tasks.description: CSM manually creates an activity log in Salesforce: type (call), date/time, account, participants, summary of discussion, outcome, follow-up actions. Also updates account health score manually if it has changed.
tasks.automation_notes: Call metadata (participants, time, duration) and email content can be captured automatically. Structured summary can be AI-generated from call notes.
tasks.error_notes: CRM logging is the most commonly skipped or abbreviated task. When CSMs are busy, this slips. Estimated 15-20% of interactions are not fully logged, creating gaps in account history.

integration_gap.description: Outlook email activity is not synced to Salesforce. CSMs must manually log each email interaction as a CRM activity. Calendar meetings are also not auto-logged. There is no automatic capture of communication events.
integration_gap.downstream_assembly_work: 11 min per escalation × 18 escalations/month = 198 minutes/month per CSM
integration_gap.data_quality_impact: ~17% of interactions missing from CRM account history. This creates blind spots for CSM handoffs, renewal risk assessment, and management reporting.

tasks.name: Create follow-up tickets in Zendesk
tasks.description: CSM creates 1-3 follow-up tickets in Zendesk: one for the 3-day check-in, one for the 14-day resolution confirmation, and one for the 30-day satisfaction check (for high-priority cases).
outputs: Zendesk follow-up tickets with due dates and ownership
tasks.automation_notes: Follow-up ticket creation is deterministic (fixed intervals, fixed template content). Can be auto-triggered when an escalation is marked resolved.
tasks.error_notes: CSMs sometimes create only the 3-day ticket and forget the 14-day and 30-day. Estimated 25% of cases missing at least one follow-up ticket.

integration_gap.description: Follow-up tickets created in Zendesk are not reflected in the CSM's Outlook calendar or Salesforce task list. CSMs must manually remember or check Zendesk. Scheduling follow-up calls is a separate manual step in Calendly.
integration_gap.downstream_assembly_work: 9 min per escalation × 18 escalations/month = 162 minutes/month per CSM

tasks.name: Schedule follow-up calls in Calendly
tasks.description: CSM manually creates Calendly links or sends calendar invites for the 3-day and 14-day check-in calls. Logs the scheduled calls in a personal Google Sheet tracker.
outputs: Calendly booking links or calendar invites
Entry in CSM's personal tracking sheet
tasks.automation_notes: Scheduling based on fixed intervals is fully deterministic. Calendly links can be generated automatically. Personal tracking sheet is a symptom of missing CRM-scheduling integration.

integration_gap.description: Calendly bookings do not automatically create Salesforce activities or Zendesk follow-up tickets. The scheduled call exists in Calendly and in the CSM's personal Google Sheet, but not in the official systems of record. When a CSM handles 18+ escalations per month, this personal tracker becomes unmaintainable.
integration_gap.downstream_assembly_work: 8 min per escalation × 18 escalations/month = 144 minutes/month per CSM
integration_gap.data_quality_impact: Personal Google Sheet tracking is abandoned or falls behind in high-volume periods. Management has no visibility into upcoming follow-up obligations.

tasks.name: Update account health score in CRM
tasks.description: After resolution, CSM manually adjusts the account health score in Salesforce based on their subjective assessment of how the escalation went and the current relationship state.
tasks.automation_notes: Mechanical inputs to health score (ticket volume, response times, feature adoption) can be automated. The qualitative relationship signal (e.g., 'the VP sounded like she was shopping competitors') should remain human-entered.

workflow_summary.note: These percentages are for a single 'ideal' case. In practice, pre-task assembly (context gathering) and post-task administration (logging, scheduling) often bleed into each other and account for the 64% assembly figure in the CSM's time tracking data.
